title: Product Marketing Alignment
summary: The meeting covered developer plugins, internal account issues, and critical third-party security concerns like the Axios Trojan. Updates included website restructuring with a new resource center and Matt Watson's personal OS project, aiming for an organizational conversational CRM. Agent architecture and Conserver monetization were also discussed.

decisions.description: Matt Watson will pass the Axios Trojan warning to development channels.
Conserver monetization will be based on managed solutions and proprietary features.

actionItems.task: Check with Herenda for updates on the team account and premium access.
Check with Sergey for unblocking Snowflake access.
Pass the Axios Trojan warning to development channels.
Rotate all API keys if an infected Axios version is found.
Migrate Docker-intensive local development to Mac Mini.

tensions: Questions regarding the team account's 5-hour limit and push towards enterprise setup.
General security risk of arbitrary plugins and code, even commented-out in markdown.
Challenges of governance and safety with widespread common dependencies like Axios.
Matt Watson's confusion about trusting large models to architect agent systems correctly.
Paulo Trovao's initial confusion about Conserver monetization for an open-source product.
referencesToPast: Michael mentioned a prior issue shared with Matt regarding the 5-hour limit.
Michael referenced a busy day preventing an update to Paulo on Snowflake access.
Paulo referred to 'the jQuery days' and people putting scripts in it.
Michael mentioned Umba being early adopters of Node.
Matt recounted an experience with CDK data access while operating a big dealer group.

contributions.description: Found Superpower and Context 7 plugins helpful for development.
Sequential thinking plugin slows process, increases bug fixing success rate.
Observed team account structure seems designed to push users toward enterprise setup.
Warned about a Trojan in Axios versions 1.4.1 or 1.14.1.
Emphasized general security risk of 'willy-nilly plugins' and arbitrary code.
Updated on completed website restructuring, resulting in a snappy resource center.
Detailed a personal OS side project for ingesting signals and automating actions.
Vision for scaling personal OS to an organizational conversational CRM for VCON.
Suggested separating concerns with specialized agents for different functions and models.
Explained that only the orchestrator can spawn parallel sub-agents, not sub-agents themselves.
Using post-session feedback to refine Claude's handling of ingested data for agents.
Clarified Conserver monetization is based on managed solution and proprietary features.
Plans to migrate Docker-intensive local development to a Mac Mini for better performance.
